message:
Welcome to The Unknown Unknown, are you prepared for the challenge?

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands* 
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Hey team, getting a few reports now, looks like confusion from the customers on what they ordering?
This is strange
Just seen a couple of reports of customers not receiving the right item on delivery
Hmm? that sounds strange. @stuck2-bloomberg.net can you take point and start sizing this up?
Hi everyone, I am here and ill take point indeed.
:star: Take command
Thanks Stephen
@bob is there any pattern you are seeing?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
Checking reports...
Let me know if you need anything from me
One report said the user wanted to buy 'Sunglasses' but he got 'Tank-top' in confirmation
If I click on sunglasses in the webpage I get a picture of a mug
I can reproduce
:star: Reproduces the incident
Just double checked with shipping looks like the items that has been ordered are correct and we're sending the right item
We have currently got more than 17 complaints so far
this may be an issue with the frontend then
@daniel can you review if any frontend changes have been made
:star: Reviewed changes
Sure, but what's the issue ?
@Stephen - Do we know if we are taking orders based on label or image ? we may take wrong orders
customers are receiving unexpected items and I can reproduce that the image for sunglasses was incorrect on drilling
Here's the detailed info on each of the changes made today
:star: Qualify the problem
Hope this helps:https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
Okay, do we haev an incident ?
@daniel can you send me a link to the recent releases please
https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/MDI1YjRiNzUxMGEwNGVmMjkwNmU2Y2ZkNzRjZjBjMWE
We could try to halt all incoming orders but not sure on the scale of impact
@stuck2-bloomberg.net Could you please discuss this with Bez?
I am going to call this an incident as it reduces trust in our product and is impacting clients directly
If we're taking the wrong orders, then let's pause all incoming orders right away. We can't afford huge returns or refunds
@bob on it
@bez agreed
@daniel yes let's halt all orders
Thanks @Stephen - Let me know if you need any help from me
Whenever needed, we can switch the site to maintenance mode via a feature-flag update in the config repo. It cleanly pauses new orders once business gives the go-ahead
What's the impact?
*Incident Number INC100070:* SEV1 incorrect orders stopping sales
:star: Incident record created
How big is this problem?
@bez can you list your approval to halt all orders
Getting up maintenance banner on the website...
I've made this a sev1 as we have halted the ability to sell
we need all hands here
Ok the website now displays a maintenance banner
@stuck2-bloomberg.net How does it look now?
I haev reviewed all PROD changes - There is nothign wrong in them - Shall we start looking health and errors
as we've paused now we've limited direct impact for erroneous orders but obviously we are paused
@stuck2-bloomberg.net Now that we are under maintenance. Let's prioritize resolution
Thanks , thats a good move
@daniel i see the maintenance banner but that is all
can we unlock the webpage internally so we can investigate please
Yeah - thats the quickest we could do else we would take too many wrong orders
We cannot unlock internally, we dont have that option , sorry
@tanya @shay you both have recent releases, can you please review if they can be reverted
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
@STephen - we are gettign too may calls from customer, can we expedite the resolution ?
@daniel what other methods do we have to confirm a fix
I haev reviewed my change on PROD
can rollback
same here we can roll back as well
@bob we are working on it, it is better to field calls about the website being unavailable vs sending out wrong orders
Thanks
i see that the banner has a 1 hour ETA
I can take a look into our services now
we should keep that in mind and review if we are likely to breach that
We don't have any alerts in the system
All apps looks healthy, no errors at all
I will take care of that if we are breaching - we can extent
Even i don't see any issue
Must be network or platform issue.
in the grafana there are severe frontend logs
:star: Facts over opinion
@daniel who can look into those logs
Thats because we have brought down the front end right now
App health looks alright
Checking now...
no memory or cpu spikes all looks good from Infra nothing to highlight
Although I don't have any data, I strongly believe this issue originates from the application rather than the platform.
@maya @hamed we need to review all angles on this please
No spikes or response time deviations, app owners must check configuration.
This is not an infra problem. If users are seeing mismatched products, someone could be messing with our system otherwise its an application/service-level bug
But it's not a platform issue.
Sure, i ll do a quick security scan
@daniel i mean the logs before we brought them down
The issue could be anywhere, we should look at the configuration mapping between product images and names.
@Daniel do you know which application holds this data?
@Tanya,can you check if this is stored in a database or Redis cache?
Before we brought down the front end - All are returnign 200's @Stephen
@stuck2-bloomberg.net if there are no visible errors, this might be a configuration issue; the above details may help narrow it down.
so we've rolled back some recent changes - but we can't check the impact via the webpage
are there other tests we can run?
The configuration is stored in the Product Catalogue Service. I pushed a change in UAT yesterday that updates the Redis cache with the latest product names.
@Hamed yes, this information is stored in Redis cache and the cache was refreshed earlier today.
Can you send me a photo of what you are seeing wrong with the platform @stuck2-bloomberg.net
@bez we are still investigating whether it is the platform backend or frontend
we do not have a smoking gun at the moment
@Stephen, the information is stored in redis cache
I can check the database and provide a Redis key dump for product:<id>:name keys
@daneiel can you check the mappings
'Redis key dump' means extracting the stored data for specific Redis keys like 'product:<id>:name' so you can see what values are currently saved in the Redis database.
Ok here is what i found
check what is stored for sunglasses
But product_id: 3 is actually 'mug'
Where are we in the investigation? I just want to know the current working theories
It is, looks like my script has run in PROD and updated the wrong names for each product in DB
Oh no, that not good! Shall we go with a rollback?
that sunglasses: mug association matches what i say
we have a strong idea of the issue now
So, my change has mistaken taken REDIS_PROD instead of UAT
that a redis mapping is incorrect which matches my first hand experience on the website
Hence, the name and data sequence is a mismatch
@daniel can we revert to previous state
OKay , thanks- do you have all the right people in the chat ?
I think this is cache issue we should clear the cache and restart Product catalogue Service, that will make fresh calls to DB and the cache will be refreshed
No, Dan's query has updated the wrong names in DB. The data is wrong in DB therfore we will have to rehydrate the canonical database with the correct product Id names .
Do we have an ETA for the fix?
@Tanya, i think you are complicating an easy solution. A restart should fix this
the person who made the change is in the chat and I am asking them to revert to previous value
It your call @stuck2-bloomberg.net
@dan @tanya how long to refresh and restart?
:star: Manage actions and timings
we are looking into the timing of restore now
I think we will have to rehydrate the DB
Dan thinks other wise
Restrat should be fast in 3 to 5 minutes
thanks Tanya I understand that, given that the restart is 3-5 minutes we can attempt Dan's theory
@daniel how confident are you in that timing
i am quite sure
t should be even less
ok let's restart please
Thank you, i will clear cache and bounce the servers
I see the DB update itself is wrong - its wrong data populated in DB
Oh no, the data still appears to be wrong. I believe Tanya might be right
while that is happening who can prepare the sql to rehydrate the data
:star: Know your team and their roles
This will not clear the issue
ok great, we have ruled out that theory
We will have to follow below steps to Rehydrate the db.
now we can focus on correcting the data
'Rehydrate the db' means restoring or repopulating the main database with accurate data from a reliable source or backup.
do we have the previous data available?
1. **Flush** corrupted product-name keys

```bash
redis-cli --scan --pattern 'product:*:name'| xargs redis-cli del```
2. **Rehydrate** product name cache from canonical DB
3. **Restart** frontend/product-catalog-service** to refresh in memory cache
4. **Verify** product listings display correct names
I can perform Step 1 and 2. Dan or Shay has to perform step 3
@stuck2-bloomberg.net please verify that all steps have been completed
Are we good to proceed @stuck2-bloomberg.net?
@tanya please start
Thanks for go ahead, implementing step 1 now
Step 1 is now complete
@dan be on stand-by please
Moving on to step 2
restore steps in action now
Step 2 complete
Thanks, let me know when done - i will update the stake holders as well
lets proceed with the next 2 steps
Starting step 3.....
thanks both
Step 3 is complete
All the recovery steps are completed
can we do the redis dump again pleae
please
Sure, i can do that
@daniel
ok thanks @tanya
Parallelly, i ll verify other products too
thanks
verifying fix internally now
@stuck2-bloomberg.net Checked the mapping again. And this how it looks now
Always confirm service health, errors, and data testing before removing a maintenance page - it avoids surprises when traffic returns
Other products look good as well
Perfect
@tanya3:name was always mug
are we sure that sunglasses now map properly
:star: Cross checking resolution with team
Yes, we are sure
apologies have re-reviewed the dump above and this is a correction
ok anyone object to bringung up?
bringing up?
We haev taken data from canonical DB and mapped to right image and product
We are good to bring down the maintenance page
@bez we have corrected the data and spot checks look correct again
:star: Validate the fix
We are good
can we get your approval to bring site back online pleae?
@bez
Go ahead
waiting on your approval to go-live again
internal indicators are that we are good
:star: Confirm resolution with the business
Go ahead
but we cannot verify the client viewpoint dorectly
we are good
thanks @bez
@Stephen , Shall we go ahead?
@daniel let's bring back online
Maintenance page removal in progress...
We are LIVE for orders back again!
We've recovered, but a few orders are in dispute. Should we cancel them or contact customers first?
i still see hte minatenance banner
Please confirm this with Bez before we proceed
Its back for everyone now
@daniel can you confirm that you can view the webpage now?
Generally takes 30 seconds
ease check now
Please
ok I'm in now and sunglasses look correct
Great

@Stephen ? any update 
https://comms.prod.uptimelabs.io/bloomberg-net/pl/jh5q9t7isfdm98t66ectrb7nee
thanks everyone, now we are back in business, but we need to recover from the earlier problems
can we quantify the erroneous orders
Can you check with Bez, what do we do with orders taken during wrong image and labels
@bob can you quantify how many of these we have
we are back up and running
Yeah, we can correlate time and data - we have around 3000 orders during that time
Great
now we need to recover from the erroneous orders
If a customer really wants to cancel, go ahead - otherwise leave the order as is. For anything not delivered yet, have support double-check with the customer before taking action
Looks like we are back to business
I am asking Bob for stats on those
sounds good, thanks
Well done everyone
Root cause - UAT data refresh script was accidentally run against prod, overwriting product names in DB and Redis cache with test data. Monitoring missed it as health metrics stayed normal.
@Bez suggests we reach out to clients with erroneous orders - do we have enough resource to cover the 3000?
Thanks @stuck2-bloomberg.net and team. You kept the Online Boutique on track - great work under pressure
